body.title: in_progress stall detection + stall-check skill
body.result: stall_check.py (age-based in_progress detection) + /stall-check skill + context-load Step 5.5 (15 tests, 44 total passing)
body.intention: I intend that any ticket stuck in_progress for more than 2 hours is automatically surfaced as stalled so it doesn't silently block the queue.
body.description:
Tickets that enter in_progress and are never closed (CC session died, compact happened mid-sprint, worker got stuck) become invisible blockers. Need two things: (1) a /stall-check skill that lists all in_progress tickets with their age in hours, and (2) a staleness gate — either a periodic check or a context-load step — that escalates tickets >2h old to hold with a note 'stalled in_progress: check status'.

Recommended shape: /stall-check reads cc_queue for in_progress tickets, computes age from started_at (or updated_at as fallback), prints sorted by age. The gate logic runs inside context-load (Step 5) — if any in_progress ticket is >2h old, surface it with [STALL?] prefix so Akien sees it on every session open. Optionally: auto-move to hold with a canned note to force explicit re-claim.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (or a new stall_check.py), ~/.claude/skills/stall-check (new skill file), ~/.claude/skills/context-load (add stall surfacing to Step 5)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite — cc_queue uses Postgres; read-only query is safe
**Scope boundary:** In scope: /stall-check skill, stall surfacing in context-load, optional auto-hold on >2h tickets. Out of scope: changes to ticket lifecycle, new queue columns (use started_at or updated_at from existing schema).
**Completion criteria:** Running /stall-check lists in_progress tickets with age. context-load surfaces any ticket >2h old with [STALL?] label. T-credentials-split-structure (currently stuck in_progress) is immediately visible on next /context-load. Tests: unit test for age calculation logic.
